terms.definition: pratiṣṭham (neuter adjective compound) — steadily situated, immovably established; acala unmoving + pratiṣṭha foundation
attains, reaches; adhi-√gam to go toward; the destination achieved
kṛt — one who refrains from work
in the water; the sense-field, the ocean of viṣaya.
without other, exclusive; undivided attention; the mind not fragmented between desires
kāle (compound, locative) — at the time of death; anta (end) + kāla (time)
is followed/obeyed; from anu-vidh (to follow rules). The mind's voluntary submission to sensory movement.
from non-commencement, from not beginning; ārambha = beginning/undertaking
unattached, free from clinging
helpless, under compulsion
attains, reaches, obtains; from aś (to reach, attain)
sāt (भस्मसात्) — Turned to ashes. Hacker Metaphor: The terminal command rm -rf legacy_code/ — irreversible deletion of obsolete inheritance.
the ultimate beneficiary/enjoyer; the Root User who owns all processes and their outputs.
living entities, beings; literally "that which has come into existence" — all embodied souls
In Kṛṣṇa consciousness; operating within the frequency of the Divine rather than the ego.
divine, pertaining to Brahman; the state of being established in ultimate reality
nāśaḥ (compound) — destruction of intelligence; the collapse of buddhi's capacity to reflect puruṣa
discriminating intelligence; power to distinguish puruṣa from prakṛti
intelligence, discernment, the faculty of discrimination
of those that roam/wander; from car (to move). The senses in their natural uncontrolled state — not evil, just mobile.
contemplating, the mind's scanning function; first step in the chain of entanglement
aversion, repulsion; from dviṣ "to hate"; the tendency to push away unpleasant objects

The envious, the hateful; from dveṣa (hatred/aversion); those who direct rāga-dveṣa at you; the critical test of sama-buddhi is not how you treat the su-hṛt but how you regard the dveṣya — the mind’s default is retaliation; the yogī’s override is equanimity.
vidhā (compound, nom. sg. f.) — two-fold, of two kinds; dvi (two) + vidhā (manner/kind)
this; referring to the previously described state
terrible, dreadful, fierce; the quality of the battlefield
by the modes (sattva, rajas, tamas)
destruction, cessation; not suppression but natural dissolving when cause is removed
arthān — objects of the senses
from the objects of the senses. What is restrained is contact with viṣaya, not the senses themselves.
the five senses of perception (jñānendriyas)
better, superior; the comparative of jyāya (elder/greater)
yoga (masculine compound) — the yoga of knowledge; jñāna (knowledge) + yoga (union/discipline)
The fire of knowledge. Hacker Metaphor: A secure-scrub utility—it doesn’t patch flaws, it erases the entire operating system and leads you to bare-metal truth.
having known/understood; the debug output that reveals the true system architecture.
is awake, is alert; from jāgṛ to wake, to be vigilant
yoga (masculine compound) — the yoga of action; karma (action) + yoga (discipline)
The yoga of action. The process of transforming mundane work into spiritual service by offering the results to the Divine.
by the organs of action
the five organs of action (hands, legs, speech, genitals, anus)
O Kṛṣṇa; keśa (hair) + va (possessor); literally "one with beautiful hair"
anger; from kru "to be crooked"; the ego's defense when desire is frustrated
kāmī (masculine noun compound) — one who desires to fulfill desires; the tautology reveals the futility
desire, lust; from kam "to wish"; attachment extended into active seeking
is compelled
moment, instant

Mediator, neutral party; madhya (middle) + stha (standing); the one who stands in the middle; not passive but positioned — the mediator chooses the center, which is itself a stance; the yogī’s sama-buddhi mirrors this position: standing at the center of all polarities without being pulled to either pole.
the Supreme Lord; the kernel-level process that manages all system resources.
by the mind
paraḥ (compound) — having Me (mat-) as the highest/ultimate (paraḥ); single-pointed devotion
thought, opinion, doctrine; from man (to think)
pretender, hypocrite
delusion, bewilderment, loss of discrimination; from muh (to become confused)
freedom from reaction, inaction in action; the state of being akarma
restrained, controlled; from ni-grah (to hold down, check). Not suppression but directed holding.
devoid of false ego; ahaṃkāra = "I-sense," nir = without; the root of bondage cut
without sense of "mine"; mama = mine, nir = without; no appropriation
extinction, liberation; nir + vāṇa (blowing out)
regulating, controlling
you cause to engage, you employ; from yuj (to yoke)
to be decided, to be determined; definite, decisive; from ni-ci (to ascertain completely)
night, darkness, state of unknowing; the condition where consciousness is obscured
free from desire; from nir + spṛh (to wish); natural state without craving
The ultimate, unsurpassed good; liberation from the cycle of birth and death (saṁsāra).
firm standing, devotion, faith, fixed concentration; from ni-sthā (to stand firmly)
boat, vessel; the mind as vehicle. Requires direction or drifts.
Constrictive yoga; a cosmic brace that aligns the spinal column and turns obstacles into purification fuel.
becomes firmly established; pari (completely) + atiṣṭhate (stands firm)
to serve with devotion, to worship; from pari (completely) + upāsana (sitting nearby/residing in)
Pure/Sublime. Hacker Metaphor: Single-level entropy—no fragmentation, no bugs, executing in constant time.
of one who sees, of the seer; from paś to see — the sage whose perception is purified
wisdom, intelligence; from pra + jñā (to know). The capacity for discrimination — the CPU of consciousness.
jaiḥ — born of material nature
agitating, turbulent, violently active; from pra + math, "to churn violently"
forcibly, violently, by force; the senses drag the mind against its will
cetasaḥ (adj.) — of the satisfied/happy mind; prasanna = placid, clear, satisfied
grace, mercy, serene clarity; from pra-sad "to settle down"; the settled state of citta when vṛtti ceases
the settled state of grace; when citta settles into the ocean of consciousness; the prerequisite for hāniḥ
well-founded, established, rooted; wisdom becomes stable like a tree with deep roots
established, founded; from prati-sthā (to stand firm). Stability achieved, not stability sought.
is lost, falls; the terminal state of the kleśa cascade
the person, the soul, the conscious self; also the Supreme Person
By sinful action; here referring to any action performed with the intent of personal gain (ego-driven), which creates binding karma.
The "Early Autumn" nakṣatra; symbolizes the peak of creative luxury and the need for detachment.
The "Early Rain" nakṣatra; symbolizes the flow of will being redirected from the ego to the Absolute.
attachment, coloring toward; from raj "to color"; the tendency to grasp at pleasant objects
that person; he

Equal intelligence; sama (equal/same) + buddhi (intelligence/discriminative faculty); not "equal feeling" — feelings can lie; buddhi is the discriminative faculty that precedes feeling; when buddhi holds all categories as one, feeling follows; the yogī doesn’t suppress emotion — he restructures the cognitive architecture that generates it.
one fully attains; sam + adhi + gam (to go toward completely)
perfect delusion, complete bewilderment; total loss of orientation in matter
ocean, the gathering-place of waters; the complete reservoir
from renunciation; sannyāsa = complete renunciation of world and works
The act of renunciation. In its lower form, it is the physical abandonment of duties; in its higher form, it is the mental abandonment of the sense of proprietorship.
in all respects, completely. Total restraint, not partial.
Integration; the combined state of concentration, meditation, and absorption that allows the yogi to see through the illusion of proprietorship.
having controlled, restraining
restraining, unifying, coalescing; gathering the scattered senses into one direction
one who practices self-control; literally "one who holds together" — holds the mind from its natural tendency to fragment
Attachment; the "sticky" quality of the mind that binds the soul to the results of action.
attachment, binding; from saṅj "to join"; the merge point where buddhi fuses with prakṛti
perfection, success, accomplishment; the goal of yoga
thinking of, dwelling upon
vibhramaḥ (compound) — bewilderment of memory; the native memory of spiritual identity becomes distorted
state, position, establishment; from sthā (to stand)
the benefactor/well-wisher; the system administrator who ensures the environment is stable for all processes to run.

Well-wisher by nature; su (good) + hṛt (heart); the person whose heart naturally wishes you well — not through obligation or contract but through disposition; this is the deepest category of ally because it operates below consciousness.
of the Sāṅkhyas, the empiric philosophers; followers of Sāṅkhya
therefore, from that; logical connector. BG 2.67's destruction leads to 2.68's establishment.
but; on the other hand; contrasting particle
is called

Equanimity, overlooking, non-reactive awareness; from upa (near) + īkṣ (to look at); to look closely at something without reacting; not avoidance — attentive neutrality; you see the non-virtuous person clearly, you understand their condition, and you withhold the reactive judgment that would destabilize your own citta.
I carry, I bear; the Lord as the personal porter of the devotee's needs
having abandoned, having given up; the active completion of renunciation
becomes deluded; vi-muh (to be thoroughly confused); negated—never deluded
liberated, free from; vi-mukta — completely released from the binding force
deluded, bewildered
possessed of discrimination, wise; from vi + paśya, "seeing clearly"

Is distinguished, surpasses, excels; from viśiṣ (to distinguish); the yogī doesn’t merely achieve equanimity — he surpasses the entire framework of comparison; the verb is passive because this surpassing is not the ego’s achievement but the natural consequence of buddhi’s restructuring.
is by far superior; distinguished
mixed, confused, contradictory; from vi-ā-miśr (to mix thoroughly)
by speech, by words; from vac (to speak)
endeavoring, struggling; one who makes effort toward self-control
one who; the subject being described
kṣema (noun) — yoga = attainment; kṣema = preservation; combined, the complete care of the devotee's welfare
of the yogīs, of those devoted to yoga practice
yoked, united; one who has accomplished yoga; the conquered mind
Suppression of disturbances; the process of overcoming mental modifications.
rise, predominance; the upsurge of irreligion's influence over consciousness.
rising up, predominance, victorious emergence; the error state becoming default
Disciplined practice; repeated application of the mind toward the goal. YS 1.13: "the effort to be fixed in concentrating the mind." Hacker parallel: Continuous integration—daily incremental commits.
pratiṣṭham (compound adjective) – "immovable foundation" – acala (unmoving) + pratiṣṭha (standing firm). The root access of consciousness that no prakṛtic process can revoke.

unbreakable, uncuttable, indivisible. The puruṣa is not composed of parts and cannot be fragmented. Unlike prakṛti which undergoes constant transformation, the soul remains integral. Hacker metaphor: the soul is the Read-Only memory that cannot be partitioned, formatted, or corrupted by any process.
very soon, without delay, quickly. The immediacy of knowledge-peace once conditions are met. Instant cache hit after proper indexing.
of irreligion, that which violates natural law
attains; obtains; adhi (over) + gam (to go) — goes beyond/achieves
attains, reaches, comes to understand; the active acquisition of prasāda through right practice—this is not passive grace but earned through discipline.
standing upon, residing in
cetāsā — "with consciousness fixed on the self" — the cognitive mode where awareness acknowledges its source in the Divine, like a wave knowing it is water.

unburnable, that which cannot be consumed by fire. Even at cosmic dissolution (mahā-pralaya) when Agni devours the elements, the soul persists. Hacker metaphor: the soul runs on a separate power grid; no amount of heat or overload can shut down the system.
those who have not ended their lives; the living; a- (negative) + gata (gone) + asūn (life).

"I-maker" (aham + kāra) — Ego-function creating identification, appropriating experience as "mine." Self-referential pointer mistaking traced memory address for source of consciousness. Code analogy: Recursive function calling itself until stack overflow.
unborn; literally "not-born," the atemporal nature of puruṣa
non-action, inaction, renunciation without qualification; refers to false renunciation of the immature practitioner
Imperishable, unlimited, undiminishing. The happiness of the self-realized person never decreases.
liberation, immortality, nectar-state; a-mṛta (without death) — not longevity but freedom from the birth-death cycle — 'root access' to eternal existence
having no other; exclusive devotion without rival; the absolute singularity of bhakti
impermanent, non-eternal; fleeting by nature, like all phenomena arising from prakṛti; the truth that all vṛttis are temporary processes
"impermanent, non-eternal" — the defining quality of all prakṛtic phenomena; that which cannot be relied upon for lasting satisfaction
you are lamenting; you are mourning; from anu-śuc, to grieve after, to follow with sorrow.

unavoidable, inalienable. From a (not) + pari-hṛ (to leave, abandon). That which cannot be opted out of, cannot be bypassed. Hacker note: You can't rm -rf the cycle. Learn to execute without friction.
"not disturbed," lit. "un-struck"; the puruṣa remains unaffected by prakṛtic activity. Hacker: a process running at kernel level that cannot be interrupted by user-space signals.
is capable of, has the capacity to; root √arh (to merit, deserve, be worthy). Absolute impossibility: na kaścit kartum arhati — "no one can [do this]."
Not attached, unattached. In BG 5.21, asakta-ātmā refers to one whose mind is not attached to external sense objects.
Detachment: Operating without the hooks of expectation. Recognition that the actor and the fruits are in different categories, even while the action continues.
without attachment, detached. Literally "not stuck to." The action complete in itself, not grasping at fruits.
non-attachment; engaging fully while recognizing results belong to prakṛti, not puruṣa; action unburdened by self-image demanding outcomes
"if, however" — introduces alternative viewpoint; the pivot from previous arguments to new consideration
remains (from ava-√śiṣ, to remain); the unitive remainder
indestructible; not capable of being destroyed. The soul cannot undergo the transformation called death because it was never born into the category of things that can die.
unmanifest, invisible, latent; the subtle seed-state before material manifestation emerges from prakṛti; like source code before compilation.
inexhaustible, without depletion. Unlike prakṛti's transformations which expend their guṇas, the soul is not diminished by what it witnesses.
imperishable self; transcendental body never deteriorating
of one who is not connected; the unyoked mind, unstable by definition, executing without proper environment variables set
not worthy of lamentation; that which should not be grieved over. From a- (negative) + śocya (worthy of lamentation).

Impurities, contamination. The obstacles that obscure the lamp of knowledge — the residues of avidyā, asmitā, rāga, dveṣa, and abhiniveśa. The five kleśas are the background code that prevents the system from booting cleanly.
terms.roots: a- (prefix negation) + śuddhi (purity, from śudh, to cleanse)

terms.definition: Liberated from bondage; the deletion of the karmic link between action and actor.
they worship, serve; related to bhakti, the verb of loving devotion
devotee; one who is devoted (from bhaj, to serve). The active condition: engagement.
by devotion; the instrument of spiritual connection
sāt — turned to ashes; complete erasure of legacy karmic debt.
fear, anxiety, dread of unpleasant; binding through avoidance
The outputs of sensory stimulation; the temporary "highs" produced by material interaction
the enjoyer; the ultimate recipient of all action
fixed mind, steady consciousness; the capacity to maintain unwavering focus; the mental state that survives input fluctuations
created being, elemental manifestation; from √bhū, "to become, to come into being"—the evolutes of prakṛti.
"created beings, elements" — from √bhū (to be, to become). All things that come into existence through the cycle of manifestation.
bhūtaḥ (adj) - Having realized the Absolute; the state of being fully absorbed in the self, free from material desires and fears.
Union with the Absolute through concentration. The state where the mind is fixed on Brahman, leading to inner satisfaction.
placing in the Absolute; ownership transfer of the result.
nāśa (m. compound) — destruction of intelligence; loss of discriminative capacity.
"intelligence, discernment": the interface between puruṣa and prakṛti. When pure sattva dominates, capable of recognizing the soul's true nature.
intelligence, discriminative capacity; the faculty that distinguishes puruṣa from prakṛti
transcendental intelligence, discriminating wisdom; the faculty that distinguishes puruṣa from prakṛti; the debugger of consciousness
intelligence, discernment, faculty of judgment; the interface between puruṣa and prakṛti; the tool that can recognize its own source
One who possesses Viveka (discernment) and can debug the illusion of temporary pleasure
"they cut" — splitting, breaking apart; the soul cannot be fragmented
thinking of, meditating upon; continuous thought-flow; unbroken remembrance
Divine, belonging to the gods. The energy that is transcendental and governing.
the embodied one, the soul; literally "possessing a body"; distinct from deha (the body)
the embodied one; consciousness inhabiting material form
reestablishing cosmic order; restoring original source code
of cosmic order, natural law; the supporting structure of existence
fixed, certain, constant, unshakeable. The pole star, Dhruva, is literally "the fixed one." Hacker note: Immutable constants. Death is const mortality = true; it doesn't take parameters.
while contemplating, pondering; the first vṛtti that externalizes consciousness.
steady, patient, firm; from √dhṛ (to hold, carry, support) — one who holds steady under pressure — the carrier of spiritual weight without system collapse
divine, transcendental; of the nature of divinity, not material manifestation
Difficult to overcome, cross, or traverse. That which is hard to conquer or pass beyond.
pain, distress, suffering; from dus- (difficult) + kha- (space/ground) — "hard ground," existential dissatisfaction — the 'error state' of material existence
miscreant; running corrupted gunas-code, unaware of infection
pairs of opposites; hot-cold, pleasure-pain, honor-dishonor — prakṛti's oscillating expressions that disturb the unsteady mind — the 'binary toggling' of material existence
aversion; the push away from unpleasant objects; the flip side of rāga, equally binding
aversion, hatred, repulsion; the inverse attachment—pushing away what caused pain; both rāga and dveṣa bind equally, just different polarities of the same trap.
hateful, odious; one who is to be hated
The eleventh day of the lunar fortnight, a day prescribed for fasting and increased devotion
those whose lives have ended; the dead; gata (gone) + asūn (life, breath).
decline, depletion; the state when dharma has fallen below operational threshold.
decline, deterioration, erosion; when peak performance fades

"strand, quality" — Three constituent qualities of prakṛti: sattva (lucidity), rajas (activity), tamas (inertia). All material phenomena are combinations of these three. Code analogy: Three basic system states — processing, clarity, stasis.
Consisting of the three modes (sattva, rajas, tamas). The material nature in its conditioned state.
when the body is being killed; present participle emphasizing the simultaneity of body's death and soul's persistence
destruction, loss, cessation; the uprooting of suffering at its root
destruction, cessation, disappearance; the loss or removal of something unwanted; spiritual garbage collection
by the senses; the five senses of perception (jñānendriya) and five of action (karmendriya)
from sense objects; indriya (sense) + artha (object/meaning); the domain where senses seek engagement
kāma-dhuk — bestower of desired objects; iṣṭa = desired, kāma = objects, dhuk = bestower
birth, appearance; in this context, the Lord's divine descent, not material birth

"birth." In classical Sāṅkhya: not the origin of the soul (which is eternal), but the assumption of a new instrument — the body-mind complex — through which puruṣa falsely identifies as agent and enjoyer. The phala (fruit) of prakṛti's cycle, binding until knowledge dissolves the bond.
conquered senses; mastery over the impulses of mind and body
"better, superior" (from jya, venerable): the higher path. Like a viveka-seeker debugging two approaches, seeking the optimal spiritual algorithm.
superior, better, more excellent; the moral hierarchy between performed duty and renunciatory evasion
better, superior; from ji (to conquer). Comparative advantage: right work trumps attempted transcendence.
tapasā — by the austerity/heat of knowledge; purification through discriminative understanding
knowledge, wisdom, especially spiritual knowledge of the Absolute Truth. The mature fruit of yoga practice. Root access to reality's source code.
the self-realized souls; those who possess knowledge as direct perception.
the fire of knowledge; the ultimate debugging tool that removes the illusion of separation.
knowledge (from √jñā)
having known, completed action

of one who has taken birth; from jan to be born, produced, arise. In Sāṅkhya: manifestation of effect from its subtle cause. Hacker note: Like a process that's spawned — once running, it has lifecycle: init, execute, terminate.
worn out, old, decayed; the material body after utility is exhausted
worn out, old, decayed; from jṝ to decay; obsolete
at any time; temporal marker emphasizing eternity beyond past/present/future
is considered eligible, fit, suitable; from √kḷp (to be able) — the technical term for 'qualified practitioner' — having the prerequisites for liberation
yoga (n.) — "union through action": work without attachment to results. The active complement to jñāna-yoga's contemplative path.
"action, work" (from kṛ, to do): has layers—fruitive (binding), prescribed (purifying), karma-yoga (liberating). Requires viveka to discriminate between them.
action, work; derived from kṛ (to do). System state: prakṛti's perpetual motion.
action, work, ritual performance; here specifically niyata-karma: duty performed without attachment to result
by the active sense organs; the faculties of work and expression (hands, feet, voice, etc.) | your I/O devices configured for output mode

"doer, agent" — Subject performing action. In reality, only prakṛti through guṇas is agent. Soul claims role erroneously. Code analogy: User thinking they compiled code when compiler did all work — they just pressed return.
son of Kuntī (Arjuna); the jīva with discriminative capacity.
"O son of Kuntī / O descendant of Bharata" — appellations invoking Arjuna's maternal and paternal lineages, reminding him of his duty to uphold dharma
"they cause to moisten" — water's capacity to penetrate; the soul is impenetrable
anger, wrath, defensive reaction; destructive emotional response
anger, wrath; rajas at peak turbulence when kāma frustrated.
"in the course of great time"; entropy affects even strongest lineages
kāmī (compound) — one who desires desires, the desirer of fulfillment. Pleonastic construction emphasizing compulsive pursuit — trapped in recursive wanting.
desire, longing; one of the five kleśas; disturbance from rajas-clouded buddhi.
duty, what must be done. Not optional. The non-negotiables of your dharma.
duty, obligation; action from structure vs. inclination (kāma)
tortoise; the eternal symbol of deliberate withdrawal. Not rejection — retractability. Hacker metaphor: hot-swappable modules: attached when needed, detached when not.
preservation of what is possessed; protection, safety; what keeps one from falling
bhāvam — My state of being, transcendental love; the Lord's own prema-bhāva
bāhu (adj.) — "mighty-armed" — ironic address; Arjuna's physical might contrasts with mental weakness
mayā — absorbed in Me, made of Me; mind saturated with Kṛṣṇa-consciousness

by the mind; the critical faculty that directs attention and serves as the reins controlling the senses | hacker metaphor: manas is your control bus; without it, the peripherals (senses) flood the CPU with garbage data

"remembering within the mind"; mental dwelling upon sense-objects even during physical restraint. System view: This is the background process consuming your CPU cycles while you think the main thread is suspended. Real saṃyama requires process termination, not just pausing the GUI.

"thinks, imagines" — From man, root of mind. Ego's primary activity: mental fabrication, constructing narratives of authorship. Code analogy: Frontend displaying dashboard user thinks represents causation, when actually just logging events generated elsewhere.

ācāraḥ (masculine compound) — false practice; conduct that appears spiritual but lacks internal substance. The root: mithyā (illusion) + ācāra (practice). Compare to mayā — the simulation rendering as real. This is the deadliest vṛtti: not knowing that you don't know your manas is still running while your body poses in meditation.
saṅgaḥ — liberated from attachment; unbound by results.
attains Me; personal attainment of the Supreme Person
wind, air, Vāyu; from √mṛ (to die, fade). Prāṇa's mobile aspect.
"the wind" — storm gods; principle of desiccation and scattering
sparśāḥ (masculine plural, compound) — "sense-object contacts" — the interface where consciousness meets prakṛti; the API boundary where input triggers response
The Lord's divine energy that manifests the material world. Often translated as "illusion" but more accurately as the divine energy that appears as duality.
death, cessation. From mṛ to die, decay. The dissolution of form. In Sāṅkhya: return of effect into cause — manifestation contracts to latency. Not annihilation of soul; retirement of garment.
"not" — repeated four times for absolute negation; neti neti in action
never takes birth; the denial of causal origination for the ātman
"not sticking"; the status of a process that generates no logs.
"you do not deserve" — the verdict: grief is beneath the station of a warrior
does not attain; negation of return, cessation of the cycle
"scattered, lost"; access severed, not truth destroyed
annihilation, dissolution, death; the return to unmanifest state; not destruction but transformation.
restrained, curbed down; from ni- (down) + grah (to seize); the senses actively withdrawn from objects like a skilled administrator recalling agents from the field
Free from dualities; the state of zero oscillation between attraction and repulsion.
"without mine-ness" — executing actions without claiming proprietorship. The cashier counting millions for his employer.
"without expectation" — acting without attachment to specific results. The sky doesn't rain expecting gratitude.
abhiyuktānām (gen. pl.) — of those always fixed in devotion; perpetual application
regulating, bringing under control; establishing discipline over | ossify system governors to throttle resource usage
prescribed, regulated, bound by cosmic/dharmic order; duty not chosen but inherited through circumstance and station
prescribed, ordained, regulated; from ni-yam (to restrain, regulate). Compiled binary of duty — specific, non-negotiable code for your system.
The Supreme: For the bhakta, the Personality of Godhead; for the jñānī, liberation. The destination reached when attachment to results is traded for connection to source.
"disciplic succession"; unbroken chain of transmission, like git history preserving truth across generations

The disciplic succession; the traditional method of transmitting spiritual knowledge from guru to disciple. Unlike academic knowledge, transcendental wisdom must be received through this living chain.
"O scorcher of enemies"; Arjuna's epithet indicating capacity to burn obstacles
lamentation, excessive grieving, crying out; the emotional reaction fired by ahaṅkāra's misidentification with temporary forms.
"lamentation, excessive wailing" — grief based on the delusion of loss; theatrical mourning. Kṛṣṇa's question is rhetorical: when did you ever actually lose anything?
"inquiry from all sides"; submissive questioning aimed at understanding, not debate.
deliverance, rescue; the Lord's primary mission is saving, not destroying
becomes well established, stands firm; pari- = completion, ava-sthā = to stand; the intelligence that attains stable, immovable discernment
stands firm, is established; pari (around/fully) + ava-sthā (to stand)
worship properly from all sides; complete, adequate worship surrounding the Lord
supreme, transcendental peace; the peace that results from knowledge. Not transactional calm but ontological stillness. System at idle, no background processes consuming resources.
Human ability, prowess, capacity. Not biological function but the reflection of Divine power in every act of strength, intelligence, and will. Our abilities are loans from the Supreme.
to see, perceive directly; the root dṛṣṭi carries the weight of immediate vision, not mediated by concept.
the learned, the wise, those with discriminating knowledge; from root paṇḍ—to accumulate wisdom.

Effulgence, radiance, luminosity. The inherent light that emanates from the Divine — not just photons but the conscious illumination that makes perception possible. The light of sun and moon is Kṛṣṇa's prabhā.
vādān (noun) — learned talks, words of wisdom; prajñā (discriminative wisdom) + vāda (speech, discussion).
discriminative wisdom; the steady consciousness that knows puruṣa from prakṛti. The debugger's eye that sees through the stack trace to the source.
wisdom, intelligence, discriminative knowledge; the awakened buddhi that distinguishes puruṣa from prakṛti
Lord of creatures, progenitor of all; Viṣṇu as source
generations, progeny; from pra-ja (to be born forth)

"nature, matrix" — Primordial material substance; unconscious substrate containing three guṇas. Code analogy: Operating system, the platform upon which all apps run. Not conscious — appears conscious only when reflecting puruṣa's light.
Those who have surrendered, submitted, or taken refuge. The act of complete surrender to the Divine.
cetasaḥ (gen. sg.) — of the satisfied/pure-hearted; prasanna = clear, bright, satisfied; cetas = mind/heart/consciousness; one whose inner lake reflects truth without distortion

terms.term: prasanta (प्रशान्त): Tranquility, the quietude that follows the cessation of mental fluctuations. The natural state of citta when kleśas (afflictions) are removed. Hacker metaphor: Prasanta = CPU at idle. No unnecessary processes, no thermal throttling
terms.definition: the mind is cool, responsive, and available for true computation.
would be accomplished, effected, made successful; the impossibility of bodily maintenance without engagement
would be accomplished; from pra-sidh. Execution status: without input, no output.
settled grace, divine satisfaction, clarity of consciousness; from pra (forth) + sād (to accomplish); the state of being thoroughly accomplished, established in one's true nature
grace, mercy, tranquility; causeless favor of the Lord; the result of bhakti-yoga practice; also the sanctified remnants of offering to the Lord.
the mercy/grace of the Lord; settled clarity, the end of mental oscillation; etymologically: pra (forth) + sad (to settle) — that which settles firmly
firmly established; rooted, unmoving. System status: daemon running, priority locked, uninterruptible.
established, fixed, firmly situated; from prati- (against) + sthā (to stand); intelligence grounded in its true nature
The withdrawal of the senses from their objects; the fifth limb of aṣṭāṅga yoga
withdrawal from senses; fifth limb of aṣṭāṅga yoga.
withdrawal of senses from objects; literally "drawing back toward oneself." The fifth limb of yoga where senses cease outward projection.

withdrawal of the senses from their objects; the fifth limb of aṣṭāṅga yoga. Not suppression but redirected flow — indriyāṇām following cittasya toward svarūpa. This is the firewall rule that actually works: send all sense-traffic to internal Reviewer (the ātmā) before routing anywhere.

The sacred syllable oṁ (a-u-m), the root vibration of all Vedic knowledge. The primordial sound from which all manifestation emerges, representing the triple aspect of creation, maintenance, and dissolution.
terms.roots: from praṇu (to hum, to sound) + suffix

terms.definition: one falls down, perishes; total system failure.
complete surrender, devotion as orientation of the total being toward Īśvara.
"approaching by bowing down"; complete surrender before the spiritual master. Root access credentials.
"proclaimed, spoken" — from pra-vac; implies formal transmission, not casual speech.
"received"; obtained through transmission, not self-generated
again taking birth; the cycle of saṁsāra, perpetuated by residual karma
tanaḥ — "ancient"; from purātana — from the earliest times. The paramparā stretches to the horizon.
khyāti (n.) — direct perception of the soul; the discriminative knowledge that transforms theoretical Sāṅkhya into realized truth — 'seeing the source code directly'
the oldest, primeval, ancient; predating creation itself
fire, the purifier; from √pū (to purify). The element of transformation.
"fire" — sacrificial flame; one of the five mahābhūtas
person, spirit as individual; teaching applies universally
mystery, secret; the hidden thing. In Sāṅkhya: that which requires buddhi-sūkṣma (subtle intellect) to perceive.

Taste, essence, flavor. The intrinsic quality that makes an experience meaningful — not the chemical composition but the divine relish that permeates substance. When we taste water, we taste Kṛṣṇa's essence.
attachment, passion, desire for pleasure; one of the three gates of hell (BG 16.21)
attachment; the pull toward pleasurable objects; root rañj (to color), that which colors the mind red with craving

attachment, passion, coloration of the mind; the condition where the mind is drawn toward objects based on remembered pleasure from past experiences; like legacy code that keeps executing because it worked once.
ṛṣayaḥ — "saintly kings"; rulers established in dharma, executive branch of spiritual order
vijñeyam (adj) – along-with-the-knowable; knowledge-as-object
yajñāḥ — along with sacrifices; created beings sent with institutional apparatus in place

Out of many thousands. Kṛṣṇa’s statistical frame — not hyperbole but precise observation. The path narrows at each stage. Thousands enter, one arrives. The funnel is not cruelty; it is the architecture of reality.
friend; intimate companion (from sac, to accompany). The relational condition: closeness.

darśinḥ (समदर्शिनः): Equal vision, non-discriminating perception. Not indifference — impartiality grounded in tattva-jñāna (truth knowledge). Hacker metaphor: Sama-darśin = seeing packets, not payloads. The enlightened debug the transmission of consciousness, not the temporary data it carries.
equal, same, balanced, equanimous; the quality of buddhi when sattva predominates and rajas/tamas are subdued — the 'zero-drift' state of the mind
yuktaḥ (adj) - Equipoised; the processor operating at zero thermal spikes between likes and dislikes.
equally disposed, same; without differentiation or preference
complete delusion; buddhi under dense tamas.
"ocean"; sam (together) + udra (water). The field containing all phenomena without being modified by them.
Proper execution: From the roots sam (integration) and car (to move). Not mere doing, but calibrated action aligned with dharma and timing.
perform completely. Execute fully.

The perfection of samādhi, the complete absorption of consciousness in the object of meditation. The fruit of surrender: not a technique achieved, but a state revealed when the practitioner stops grasping at fluctuations and rests in the Divine.
"having fully renounced" — complete surrender of agency, not abandonment of action. Like a symphony musician surrendering to the conductor.

eternal, perpetually existing, without origin or end. Not "forever" as extended time, but outside time's jurisdiction entirely. Hacker metaphor: sanātanaḥ is the code that runs outside the simulation — the reference frame that persists even when the program restarts.
bhūteṣu (loc. pl.) — in all living entities; sarva = all, bhūta = living being
constantly. Not when inspired. Without interruption.
constantly, without cessation; moment-to-moment discipline
"that very same" — the ancient science. Indicates identity across time: knowledge is not invented but recovered.
winds up, withdraws; from √hṛ with prefix sam- (completely). The total retraction of attention; hacker metaphor: disconnecting from the simulation without shutting down the machine.
The interface between a sense organ and a material object; the "input" that triggers a sensory reaction
To steady; to render stable; to fix firmly. Kṛṣṇa's command is active stabilization of the mental apparatus through deliberate intelligence.
one who has subdued or controlled the senses; sense withdrawal as prerequisite. The Ekādaśī principle of inner fasting. Closing unnecessary ports before security audit.
verily accepts, enters into; soul's active assumption of new embodiment per karma
the psychic glue of attachment that triggers karmic imprints.
association, attachment; psychological residue forming saṁskāras.
service; selfless action without desire for result. Knowledge embeds through engagement.

For perfection, for accomplishment. From siddhi — completion, fulfillment, the attainment of a goal. Not mere success in the worldly sense — the perfection of consciousness itself. The difference between compiling without errors and actually executing the program.

Of those who have achieved perfection. The same root as siddhaye, but now in the past participle — those who have already completed the journey. Even among these, Kṛṣṇa says, hardly one knows Him. Perfection is necessary but not sufficient.
memory; capacity to remember the Self, not ordinary recollection.

fixed, immovable, standing firm like a pillar. While mind and body fluctuate through the guṇas, the soul remains the invariant observer. Hacker metaphor: the soul is the server root directory — unchanging while all files and processes move around it.
Pure; the state of a soul that has been reformatted to read-only mode.
the well-wisher; the one who desires the good of all beings
happiness, pleasure, ease; from su- (good) + kha (space) — "good ground" — the 'success output' that still binds
saṁsthayā (adj) - Being established in the self; the state of self-absorption, independent of external conditions.
the pious; functionally: one in Kṛṣṇa consciousness (the binary check)
I manifest, I emit; not creation but making apparent what was always present.
I manifest, emit, release; NOT "create" — eternal forms become apparent
having sent forth, created; intentional construction
They cross over, traverse, or go beyond. To surpass or transcend an obstacle.
pervaded, spread throughout, extended across; from √tan (to stretch). Consciousness "stretches" to include — yet never merges with — what it illumines.
"even then, still" — the crux: regardless of philosophical position, conclusions remain unchanged
darśinaḥ — "those who see the truth"; direct perception of reality as it is.
in truth, in reality; ontologically, not superficially or philosophically
"you must tolerate/endure" — active forbearance, not passive suffering; the austere patience that maintains practice through all conditions
forbearance, endurance, tolerance; the capacity to bear opposites without disturbance; not stoic denial but viveka in action
having abandoned, given up; the voluntary release of the body at death
they will impart, teach, transmit; from upa-diś (to point out).
equanimity, indifference, overlooking; one of the four attitudes in YS I.33, specifically toward the sukha/duḥkha of conditioned existence; the emotional Neutral gear
having taken refuge, surrendered; those who have sought shelter
supreme, highest, best; from ud (up) — that which transcends level.
to speak, describe, articulate; from the root vad, implying the competent expression of what has been perceived.
I carry, I bear; the Lord's active taking of responsibility
Dispassion; strategic withdrawal of attention from objects that trigger the kleśas. Hacker parallel: Disabling unnecessary services; systemctl mask the resource drains.
Dispassion or non-attachment; freedom from craving for sense objects
"mastery, control"; from vaś (to be subject to). Not domination – the effortless sovereignty of the full.
to know with certainty; from the root vid, giving the name to the Vedas themselves; direct knowledge as opposed to hearsay.
know it, understand it. The grammar of instruction here is epistemological: this is not revealed truth to be accepted blindly, but observable truth to be recognized.
one whose nature follows regulated principles; disciplined freedom—the apparent paradox that structure enables liberation.
one who follows regulated principles; vidheya (what should be done) + ātmā (self) — the regulated self, not the rebellious self
"they knew"; direct perception, not theory

terms.term: vidyā (विद्या): Knowledge of the self, spiritual wisdom. In Sankhya
terms.definition: Yoga, vidyā is direct perception of puruṣa and prakṛti. Not to be confused with mundane scholarship. Hacker metaphor: Vidya = the source code of consciousness. Most people only see the compiled output (prakṛti); sages access the underlying architecture (puruṣa).
jvaraḥ — "without fever" — the absence of nervous mental agitation about outcomes. The body works; the mind calculates; but the grip of anxiety dissolves.
having abandoned, given up; voluntary relinquishment, not forced deprivation
mastery over the self; conquest of the lower nature

"delusion, bewilderment" — State of confusion by false identification. Confusion caused by ahaṅkāra making soul think it is doer. Code analogy: Process thinking it's kernel when actually just user-space application — complete misidentification of privilege level.
liberated, released, set free; the state of being completely untangled from the kleshas; root muc—to loosen, release.
by one who is liberated; vi (intensive) + muc (to release) — the state of having been unbound

ātmā (bahuvrīhi compound) — one whose ātmā (true self) is completely deluded (vi- intensive prefix + mūḍha). Being mūḍha is bad; being vi-mūḍha means your delusion has root privileges. You've installed maya.exe with admin access.

terms.term: vinaya (विनय): Humility, the absence of ego
terms.definition: driven assertion. From vi-√nī (to lead away), vinaya removes the practitioner from attachment to personal opinions. Hacker metaphor: Vinaya = running as a non-root user. Ego escalation (root access) leads to system corruption; humility (user mode) preserves integrity.
annihilation; removal of obstruction, not vengeance—deleting malware to restore integrity
The sun-god, deity presiding over the sun in this millennium. Represents the eye of the Lord.

Discriminative discernment, the highest form of knowledge in classical yoga. The direct perception of the distinction between puruṣa and prakṛti. When this fires, the funnel collapses to its final stage.
terms.roots: viveka (discrimination, from vi- + ic, to separate) + khyāti (perception, recognition, from khyā, to appear)

terms.definition: is distinguished, excels, is by far superior; stands out from others | optimized algorithm outperforming the naive implementation
purified heart; a heart cleansed of selfish desires
sense objects; the objects of the five senses, vi-ṣaya (that which spreads out/expands)
manifest, visible, expressed; the material form appearing in interim time; the compiled program running in the simulation.
"manifest, visible, expressed" — prakṛti under active guṇic modification, the material world as experienced. The compiled program running.

Obstacle yoga: An inauspicious combination meaning "falling away." The prompt to act with extra care, as wrong action now carries amplified consequence. Not an excuse for paralysis, but a call to precision.
saṁskṛtayoḥ (n) - The state of being fully absorbed in the self, having transcended material designations.
Function, activity, work. In the context of yoga, it refers to the active engagement of the senses and mind in the service of the self.
paraḥ (adj) - Fixed solely on Vāsudeva; the permanent call stack redirect to the Absolute.
garments, clothes; temporary coverings that protect but are not the form itself
garments, clothes; temporary user accounts the root admin creates and discards
freed, released from; past passive participle indicating completed liberation
whenever and wherever; cyclical, not linear
whenever and wherever; the cyclical trigger condition indicating divine intervention is not random but responds to systematic degradation.
sacrifice; any action performed for Viṣṇu's satisfaction.
saṁsiddhiḥ (noun) — mature perfection or accomplishment in yoga; the completed state when practice bears its fruit. Production deployment after passing all tests.
acquisition of what is lacking; what is needed for spiritual advancement
millennium after millennium; cyclical time, spiral recurrence

terms.term: yukta (adj., past passive participle from yuj)
terms.definition: yoked, connected, joined; one who is in Kṛṣṇa consciousness; the "compiled" soul whose consciousness runs on the proper kernel
may I attain, may I reach; from āp (to reach, obtain)
always being filled, constantly replenished; from ā-pṝ to fill completely
begins, undertakes
sits, remains
vaśya (compound adj.) — under self-control; the ātma as commander, not prisoner
self, soul
apāyinaḥ (masculine plural, compound) — "those that come and go" — describing sensations as temporary visitors, not permanent residents
"being filled continuously"; from ā-pṛ, to fill. Unlike the kāmī who empties with indulgence, the devotee receives without depletion.
vaśya (compound adj.) — under the control of the self (ātman + vaśya); the senses operating as api endpoints the self controls, not as rogue processes hijacking attention.
Myself; the Personal Absolute, not impersonal force but conscious descent.
Myself, personally; direct presence, not delegation
that which causes astonishment; the marvelous; the incomprehensible wonder that shatters ordinary perception.
wonderful, marvelous; the numinous awakening bodily resonance
soon, quickly, immediately; speed of establishment when conditions are right
praṇidhāna — surrender to the Lord; highest yoga practice, giving root access to the Divine Administrator
Surrender, devotion, and offering of all actions to the Lord. Patañjali's term for the bhakti path within yoga.
"surrender to the Lord" — the yogic practice of offering all actions to the Divine. The accelerator of spiritual practice.
the Lord, the supreme controller
the highest good, ultimate benefit; comparative of śreyas (better/best)
peace, tranquility; from śam to cease, to become calm
Sound vibration, particularly the subtle sound in ether (śabdaḥ khe). The all-pervading resonance that sustains the universe — the cosmic background radiation of consciousness.
yātrā (compound noun) — bodily maintenance, journey of the body; the minimum requirement of prakṛti that cannot be evaded
"weapons" — instruments of destruction; from swords to nuclear armaments
weapons, literally "that which cuts or kills"; from √śas (to cut, injure, kill). Hacker metaphor: destructive processes targeting containers, not the runtime.
to lament, grieve. From śuc to beam with heat, burn, suffer anguish. Wasted processing power on output that cannot be changed.
faith, clarity of mind, belief in the goal. The opening condition that makes knowledge accessible. Trusting the architecture before seeing the full implementation.
"peace"; from śam (to calm). Not the absence of stimulus but the presence of completion.
peace; the absence of mental turbulence (citta-vṛtti)
peace, tranquility; not mere absence of disturbance but a positive state of fullness rooted in satisfaction of the soul
permanent, ever-existing; from śas (to leave residue), indicating that which remains when all changes cease
uṣṇa (neuter compound) — "cold and heat" — the classic paired opposites; all dvandva (pairs of opposites) that disturb the undiscriminating mind
to hear, attend to, receive through the ear; the root śru means to flow toward, indicating the receptive posture of the student.
